description.s: Implemented as JS field view (not Hob) because the field-view mechanism doesn't exist in Hob yet, and the algorithmic core (inflate, flatWidth, navigation state machine) is more natural in JS. The DOM rendering layer is essentially verbose hiccup and would be a good candidate for Hob once field-views can be authored in Hob. Modified

Hole creation (Enter/Shift+Enter), input mode with token resolution, Space to commit+continue, structure chars create/close containers, string input mode, delete, replace with prefill+select-all, undo/redo (snapshot stack, 100 limit), save via deflate→onChange. Implemented across git commits Phase 2a and Phase 2b.
). Copy/paste deferred to Phase 5. Implemented in git commit Phase 2c.
Inline autocomplete dropdown with two modes: symbol mode (2+ chars, prefix-matches symbols from current AST + SPECIAL_FORMS) and item mode (
with 150ms debounce, starred items on empty query). Keyboard nav (arrows/Enter/Tab/Escape), mouse selection, generation counter for stale async results.

Double-click to edit (leaf → replace mode with select-all, container → append child hole, hole → enter input mode). Click between siblings to insert a hole at that position — whitespace/indent elements annotated with
attributes, hover effect in editable mode. Extracted
helper for reuse across click/dblclick handlers.
names. Scope symbols are prioritized over AST-wide symbols in the dropdown with detail labels (local, param, def, defn).

A structural editor for Hob where the user manipulates AST nodes rather than characters. The editor understands Hob's structure at all times — unbalanced parentheses, malformed expressions, and syntax errors are impossible by construction.
The editor is implemented as a Hobson
— no parsing step on load, no serialization on save. The interpreter, module system, and storage are unchanged.
Every edit produces a well-formed AST. There is no parse-error state.
An experienced user can build expressions nearly as fast as typing text. The structural constraints help rather than hinder.
When the user creates a hole, the editor shows what's valid in that position — available symbols, item references, special forms. The system presents choices.
The AST is rendered to look like properly formatted s-expressions with syntax colouring.
Load and save operate on compact JSON AST (
). The .hob sidecar (pretty-printed s-expression) can be regenerated but is not involved in the edit cycle.
The compact JSON AST format (stored in
The editor needs richer nodes for selection tracking, undo, and comments. On load, compact JSON is
IDs are generated with a simple counter (ephemeral, in-memory only). On save, editor nodes are
back to compact JSON — the round-trip is lossless.
The root is a virtual container (
) holding one or more top-level forms.
Selection replaces the text cursor. Exactly one AST node is selected at all times. The selected node is highlighted with a visible border or background colour.
Any node in the AST: a leaf (
), a container and all its contents (
: faint highlight on ancestor containers, so you can see where in the nesting you are.
: when a container is selected, both its opening and closing delimiters are emphasised.
All navigation is by keyboard. The model is tree traversal.
Move to the preceding sibling in the same container. At first child, no-op.
Move to the following sibling in the same container. At last child, no-op.
Select the containing node. At root, no-op.
If selected node is a container, select its first child. On leaves, no-op.
Jump to first child of the current parent.
Jump to last child of the current parent.
Jump to the nearest ancestor that's a direct child of root.
Jump to the next hole in document order. If no holes, no-op.
but conceptually "select more"). Can be repeated to grow.
If the current selection was expanded, contract back to the previously selected child.
If in a hole's input mode, cancel input. Otherwise select the parent.
The expansion history is a stack. Each
When the user needs to enter a new value, a
appears. A hole is a visually distinct placeholder (e.g., a highlighted empty box or a dimmed
after a selected node (inserts a sibling hole after it)
before a selected node (inserts a sibling hole before it)
) — the container is created with a hole as its first child
when a container is selected (appends a child hole at the end)
to replace the selected node (the node is replaced with a hole pre-filled with its text)
When a hole is active, the editor is in
. Keystrokes go to the hole, not to navigation.
Appends to the token buffer. Autocomplete menu filters in real time.
the current token as a node. Creates a new sibling hole after it.
create a sibling hole. Exits input mode.
Cancels input. If the hole was newly created, it is removed. If replacing, the original node is restored.
If buffer is empty: creates a list with a hole inside. If buffer has content: commits the buffer first, then creates a list sibling.
Commits the current token (if any). Exits input mode. Selects the parent container. If the delimiter doesn't match the parent container type, it is ignored (prevents structural errors).
If at start of token: switches to keyword mode (characters until Space form a keyword). If mid-token: treated as regular character.
Deletes last character from buffer. If buffer is empty, removes the hole.
When a token is committed (by Space, Enter, or a structural character), the editor determines what kind of node it is:
in a hole (or when the buffer is empty).

In string mode, all characters are literal — Space doesn't commit, parentheses don't create structure. The editor shows a text input field (or a distinct visual region) where the user types freely. Standard escapes work (
again (unescaped) ends string mode and commits the string node.
inserts a newline within the string.
The editor shows a searchable dropdown of all items in the system. The user types to filter by name or ID. The list shows the item name, type, and a preview.
Select the highlighted item, insert an item-ref node
Cancel, return to normal hole input
Select the highlighted item AND create a sibling hole (fluid continuation)

Item references are displayed in the AST as a chip showing the item's name, not the raw UUID. The chip is visually distinct (e.g., a coloured pill with the item's type icon). Clicking the chip navigates to that item.
When typing in a hole, an autocomplete dropdown appears showing contextually valid options, filtered by what's been typed so far.
bindings. Determined by walking up the AST from the hole's position.
at the top of the module.
, switch to item search. But even without
, if the typed text matches an item name, it could appear as a suggestion with an
In certain positions, the editor can show richer guidance:
: likely a function call. Show functions and special forms prominently.
: likely a binding name. Show hint "name".
: alternating key/value. Show keywords prominently for keys.
: expecting a name. Show hint "name" and suppress function suggestions.
This context awareness can be built incrementally.
Beyond hole-based input, the editor supports structural operations on existing nodes.
Remove the selected node. Siblings close the gap. If the node is a container, all children are deleted too.
Replace the selected node with a hole, pre-filled with the node's text representation.
Insert a hole after the selected node (as a sibling).
Insert a hole before the selected node (as a sibling).
. The node becomes the first child. A hole is added after it.
. A hole is added for the value.
Remove the selected container, promoting its children into the parent. Only works on container nodes.
Swap the selected node with its previous sibling.
Swap the selected node with its next sibling.
The selected container absorbs the next sibling. `(a \
The selected container ejects its last child as a sibling after it.
puts the editor in "wrap mode" where the next character determines the container type. If no valid character follows within 300ms, wrap mode is cancelled.
Copy the selected subtree to the clipboard (internal AST format + plain text as s-expression).
Insert the clipboard content as a sibling after the selection. If clipboard contains text (from outside), parse it as Hob and insert the resulting AST.
Insert a deep copy of the selected node as a sibling after it.
Each mutation pushes a snapshot (or a reversible delta) onto the undo stack. The undo stack is in-memory, lost on save/reload. Limit ~100 operations.
The AST is rendered to look like properly formatted Hob source code, with structural awareness expressed through colour and subtle visual cues.
Nodes are laid out using standard Lisp pretty-printing rules:
break after the first element, with children indented:
: forms wider than ~60 characters break to multi-line.
: name and params on first line, body indented 2 spaces.
: binding vector on first line, body indented 2 spaces.
: condition on first line, then/else each on their own line, indented 2 spaces.
: each pair on its own line, indented 2 spaces.
Hiccup vectors: tag and attrs on first line, children indented.
Muted colour, matching pair highlighted on container select
Highlighted empty region with dashed border

Item references are shown as chips with the item's name (or truncated ID if unnamed) and a small type icon. Hovering shows a tooltip with the item's type and ID. Clicking navigates to that item. In the serialised output, it becomes
line comments. Comments are stored as metadata on the node they precede (or on the container they're inside).
The editor AST node gains optional
{ id, type: 'symbol', value: 'defn', commentBefore: 'Define the greeting function' }
Add/edit a comment on the selected node. Opens a text input above or beside the node.
Comments are rendered in muted italic above or beside the node.
Comments are not part of the compact JSON AST format. They exist only in the editor's inflated representation. On save, comments can be serialised as a separate metadata field (e.g.,
) or embedded in a future AST extension. Initial implementation can defer comment support entirely.
When the editor opens a code item with
Inflate each compact JSON node to an editor node with a generated ID.
is missing or malformed, show an empty editor with a single hole.
Walk the editor AST and deflate to compact JSON.
The normal save flow applies: module cache is cleared, watches fire, views re-render.
If holes remain in the AST at save time, prevent save and highlight the unfilled holes.
A narrow bar at the bottom showing:
: the type of each ancestor from root to selection. E.g.,
: the selected node's type and value.
: "NAV" for navigation mode, "INPUT" when in a hole, "WRAP..." when in wrap mode, "STRING" during string input.
A minimal toolbar (or right-click context menu) with structural operations for discoverability. Each button shows its keyboard shortcut.
) showing all available keyboard shortcuts in the current context.
The editor is a Hobson field view for
, implemented in JavaScript (the editor itself is complex infrastructure — Hob can use it once built).
Converts compact JSON AST to editor nodes. Walks the JSON array, dispatches on type (array=list,
Converts editor nodes back to compact JSON. The inverse of inflation. Strips IDs, comments, and holes (holes should be prevented from reaching save).
The root node and full AST tree
Current mode (nav, input, string, wrap, item-ref)
All mutations go through functions that update the store and push to the undo stack.
Walks the AST and produces DOM. Each node becomes a DOM element with a
attribute. Click handlers update selection. Holes render as input-capable elements.
Re-runs after every mutation. Code items are rarely more than a few hundred nodes, so full re-render should be fast enough.
listener on the editor's root element. Dispatches based on current mode:

keydown event
  │
  ├─ mode === 'nav'      → navigation keys, mutation keys, mode-entry keys
  ├─ mode === 'input'    → token building, commit on Space/Enter, structure keys
  ├─ mode === 'string'   → all chars literal, " to close
  ├─ mode === 'item-ref' → filter typing, arrow keys for list, Enter to select
  └─ mode === 'wrap'     → ( [ { to choose container type, anything else cancels
Receives the current hole's position in the AST and the typed buffer, returns a filtered list of suggestions. Sources:

Static list of special forms and stdlib functions.
statements at the top of the file for imported names.
Build the minimal editor that can load compact JSON AST, display it with syntax colouring, and support keyboard navigation.
✓ Render AST to DOM with syntax colouring and pretty-printing layout
✓ Selection model: click to select, arrow keys to navigate
✓ Breadcrumb status bar (with clickable ancestors, node info, mode indicator)
✓ Item references resolved to names via api.get(), displayed as styled chips
✓ Read-only at this stage — no mutations
Open a Hob code item, see it rendered with colour and structure, navigate by node with arrow keys.
Add the ability to create holes and enter values.
✓ Input mode: typing into holes, token resolution
✓ Space to commit and create sibling, Enter to commit and exit
✓ Save: deflate editor AST → compact JSON, write to
Create a new Hob code item, build up
entirely through structural input, save it, and see it work.
Add wrap, unwrap, move, slurp, barf, and copy/paste.
Take existing code, restructure it using wrap/unwrap/move, copy expressions between top-level forms.
Add the intelligence layer that makes the editor self-revealing.
Scope analysis (walk AST for local bindings) — deferred to Phase 5
✓ Special form and AST symbol suggestion lists
trigger, searchable dropdown with debounced async search)
Context-sensitive hints (position-aware suggestions) — deferred to Phase 5
Start typing in a hole, see relevant suggestions. Type
, search for an item, insert it as a navigable reference chip.

Phase 5: Mouse Interactions ✓

Make the editor more discoverable for mouse users.

- ✓ Double-click to edit (leaf → replace, container → append, hole → input mode)
- ✓ Click between siblings to insert hole at that position
- ✓ Visual hover indicator for insertion points (editable mode only)
- ✓ Extracted

Mouse users can enter edit mode and insert between siblings without knowing keyboard shortcuts.

### Phase 6: Scope-Aware Autocomplete ✓

Improve autocomplete with AST-based scope analysis.
walks parent chain for binding forms, fn/defn params\n- ✓ Top-level

names included
- ✓ Scope symbols prioritized over AST-wide symbols in dropdown
- ✓ Detail labels (local, param, def, defn) shown in autocomplete
Autocomplete suggests in-scope variables first with semantic labels.\n\n### Phase 7: Polish
Integration: register as the field view for
The structural editor is the default editing experience for all Hob code items. Full round-trip: open item → navigate → edit structurally → save → code runs.
Create container (commits buffer first if non-empty)
Commit buffer, close container, select parent
Delete last char / remove hole if empty
Select item, commit, create sibling hole
Out of scope for initial implementation:
: Hiccup vectors as nested box previews.
: show the result of an expression next to it. Select a form, press a key, see its value.
: select multiple nodes and apply the same operation.
: persist AST nodes to the item store for queryability (the editor's node-based representation makes this a natural migration).
